basics.name: Garrett Comes
basics.label: Space Propulsion Engineering Intern & Scientific Software Developer
basics.summary: Aerospace engineer focused on space propulsion hardware, fluid systems, and scientific software. Since 2021, I have worked at Lockheed Martin Space in Littleton, Colorado, supporting propulsion design and analysis while building independent tools in Rust and Python for CFD, orbital visualization, embedded control, reproducible build tracing, and ML sampling experiments.

work.name: Lockheed Martin Space
work.summary: Work on space propulsion systems and supporting analysis for flight and test hardware, spanning component design, fluid behavior, electromagnetic effects, and hardware verification.
responsibilities: Design and analyze propulsion hardware and fluid-system components, including valves, interfaces, and test support equipment.
Create and maintain Creo CAD models, layouts, and drawings for propulsion assemblies and related mechanical hardware.
Perform CFD and slosh analysis to study pressure losses, transient loads, propellant behavior, and system-level fluid risks.
Build magnetic simulation models and test approaches for magnetically actuated or sensed hardware, then correlate analysis with bench and system testing.
Support propulsion hardware test campaigns, requirements verification, anomaly investigation, and cross-functional design reviews with systems, manufacturing, and test teams.
achievements: Connected design, analysis, and test work across propulsion components so model assumptions could be checked against real hardware behavior.
Developed engineering software outside the day job to sharpen numerical methods, GPU visualization, embedded controls, and reproducible analysis workflows.

projects.name: diffusion-ebm
projects.description: Research prototype combining masked-diffusion language-model logits with a sparse factor-graph sampler to improve multi-mask consistency at a fixed neural-forward budget.
highlights: THRML joint sampler, hard-equality factors, and learned pairwise-factor scaffold
Pareto sweeps against Mask-Predict and top-k ancestral baselines
Python 3.12, PyTorch, JAX, transformers, marimo notebooks, and CUDA workflows

projects.name: Valurile
projects.description: Rust CFD and visualization workspace for lattice-Boltzmann flow simulation, CUDA/OpenGL interop, airfoil and cylinder obstacles, and an egui desktop interface.
highlights: LBM solver paths in Rust with CUDA acceleration work
Interactive velocity, density, pressure, and vorticity visualization
Airfoil/cylinder obstacle controls, force histories, and drag/lift plots

projects.name: repx
projects.description: Reproducible process attestation tool for supply-chain security that traces build commands with eBPF, canonicalizes file operations, and verifies Merkle-root provenance on rerun.
highlights: Aya eBPF probes plus a Rust userspace CLI
Trace and verify commands for reproducible build attestations
SLSA-oriented JSON predicate and divergence diagnostics

projects.name: spacedb
projects.description: 3D space-object database and orbital mechanics visualizer for satellites and debris using Space-Track, GCAT, DISCOS, SGP4 propagation, high-fidelity decay tools, and WGPU rendering.
highlights: Interactive Earth, satellite, orbit-track, and catalog browser
Collision, decay, and debris-value analysis commands
Rust, egui, WGPU, satkit, Nyx, ANISE, and Rayon

projects.name: FEM Live Demo
projects.description: A standalone WebAssembly finite-element simulation demo kept live on the portfolio as a lightweight browser-based engineering visualization.
highlights: Rust-generated WebAssembly delivered as a static GitHub Pages demo
Canvas-based simulation surface with no server dependency
Preserved under the same public URL during the Astro migration

projects.name: Stream Sim
projects.description: Browser-based fluid simulation demo from the current portfolio, preserved as a standalone interactive page with streamline visualization controls.
highlights: Live static demo with dashboard-style controls
Interactive flow-field and streamline visualization
Kept separate from the newer Valurile Rust CFD workspace

projects.name: Eddy Current Braking System
projects.description: Embedded controls and magnetic braking project combining Rust no_std firmware for an Arduino Due, AS5048A encoder feedback, PWM motor drive, UART telemetry, an egui dashboard, and Python/NGSolve magnetic simulation.
highlights: Feedforward and PI speed-control modes with live tuning
Magnetic encoder, H-bridge PWM, slew limits, and serial telemetry
Nix-based firmware, dashboard, and simulation workflows
